summary: Advanced matching and cleanup for the victory/vi code area by replacing several placeholder vi functions with C implementations, promoting better vi-prefixed symbol names, and updating all asm and symbols.txt references to keep relocations consistent. The PR also introduced a shared inline screen-erase helper, named animation-table and erase-color globals, deleted now-unneeded per-file static headers, and added a missing lbShadow prototype needed by the matched vi code.
what_changed: Renamed core vi symbols in symbols.txt and callsites from generic un_/fn_ names to vi-scoped names, including viGetCharAnimByIndex, vi_8031C9B4, vi_8031CA04, vi_8031CAAC, vi_8031CB00, vi_8031CC68, vi_8031CC8C, vi_8031CD20, vi_8031CD94, vi_8031D000, vi_8031D020, vi_8031D80C, vi_8031D9C4, vi_8031DC80, vi_8031E0F0, vi_8031E6CC, vi_8031E6EC, and vi_8031ED50.
Renamed the victory animation filename table from un_803FFFA8 to vi_animation_table and exposed access through viGetCharAnimByIndex.
Named two sbss erase-color globals as erase_colors_vi0102 and erase_colors_vi0501, replacing un_804D6F34 and un_804D6F7C in symbols.txt and asm references.
Added an inline vi_EraseScreen helper in vi.h that wraps HSD_CObjSetCurrent, HSD_SetEraseColor, HSD_CObjEraseScreen, vi_8031CA04, HSD_GObj_80390ED0, and HSD_CObjEndCurrent.

Implemented small vi0102 functions in C: vi_8031CC68 animates the JObj, vi_8031CC8C clears/erases via lbShadow_8000F38C and vi_EraseScreen, vi_8031CD20 animates a CObj and triggers vi_8031C9B4/lb_800145F4/gm_801A4B60 on frame conditions, vi_8031D000 calls vi_8031CAAC, and vi_8031D020 stores two s8 fields into un_804D6F60.
Implemented vi0402 functions vi_8031D80C, which animates a camera object and exits on end_frame, and vi_8031D9C4, a wrapper around vi_8031CAAC.
Implemented vi0501 functions vi_8031DC80, which performs shadow disable plus shared screen erase, and vi_8031E0F0, another vi_8031CAAC wrapper.
Renamed and implemented vi0601 entry points vi_8031E6CC, vi_8031E6EC, and vi_8031ED50, including JObj animation and vi_8031CAAC wrapper behavior.
Deleted vi0102.static.h, vi0402.static.h, and vi0501.static.h after moving needed declarations or static data ownership into normal C/header files.
Added lbShadow_8000F38C(s32) to src/melee/lb/lbshadow.h for use by the new vi erase-screen routines.
Updated non-vi asm users such as asm/melee/gm/gm_1A36.s and asm/melee/un/un_2FC9.s so data tables and branches reference the renamed vi symbols rather than stale un_ labels.
smart_moves: Centralized repeated CObj erase-screen setup into vi_EraseScreen, making multiple vi match attempts reuse the same HSD_SetEraseColor/HSD_CObjEraseScreen/HSD_GObj_80390ED0 sequence instead of duplicating fragile code.

Kept symbol renames synchronized across C declarations, assembly labels, data-table .4byte entries, sbss labels, and config/GALE01/symbols.txt; this avoids unresolved symbols and prevents stale un_ names from hiding real vi ownership.

Used descriptive names where behavior was clear, such as viGetCharAnimByIndex, vi_animation_table, erase_colors_vi0102, and erase_colors_vi0501, while retaining address-based vi_ names where the exact semantic name was not proven.
Removed static.h files only after relocating necessary types or declarations, reducing project-specific static-header clutter while preserving the un_804D6F60_t layout and static storage in vi0102.c.
Matched tiny thunk functions as simple C wrappers around vi_8031CAAC, a low-risk way to retire assembly while preserving exact call structure.

Introduced required baselib includes directly in the headers/source that use HSD_CObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_SetEraseColor, GET_COBJ, and GET_JOBJ, making the new C implementations less dependent on incidental include order.
decomp_lessons: When converting asm-referenced functions to C, update every cross-file asm call and data-table function pointer, not just the C declaration; gm_1A36.s still had victory table entries pointing at the old un_ names until this PR changed them.
Shared inline helpers can be useful in matching when several files emit the same short HSD rendering sequence, but the helper must live in a header with all needed baselib types and macros available.
Do not over-name uncertain functions: this PR gives a real behavior name to viGetCharAnimByIndex but leaves many functions as vi_8031xxxx, preserving address traceability where semantics remain weak.

Small sbss objects often become easier to reason about after naming by usage; erase_colors_vi0102 and erase_colors_vi0501 are evidenced by byte loads into HSD_SetEraseColor and stores from object data before screen erasure.
Deleting static headers is safe when their only purpose was to hold file-local declarations that can move into the implementation file or the normal public header.
Frame-condition code can match by directly using HSD_AObj curr_frame/end_frame fields from CObj/WObj/AObj chains when the struct includes are available.
naming_conventions: Use vi_8031xxxx for vi subsystem functions whose behavior is not yet semantically named.
Use a semantic vi-prefixed name when behavior is clear, as with viGetCharAnimByIndex and vi_animation_table.
Use per-source-slice names for static/shared color buffers, e.g. erase_colors_vi0102 and erase_colors_vi0501, rather than generic sbss addresses.
Keep address comments in headers for decomp navigation, e.g. /* 31D80C */ void vi_8031D80C(HSD_GObj* gobj);
Prefer meaningful parameter names once a type is known, e.g. changing arg0 to HSD_GObj* gobj in vi_8031CA04 and related prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed assembly labels without changing instruction bytes where functions remained in asm, preserving matching while improving symbol ownership.
Updated function pointer data tables in gm_1A36.s from un_8031D000/un_8031CD94/un_8031D9C4/un_8031E0F0/un_8031E6CC/un_8031ED50 to their vi_ equivalents.
Retired several placeholder `/// #symbol` stubs by writing short exact C bodies, especially animation callbacks and vi_8031CAAC wrappers.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in vi_8031CC8C and vi_8031DC80, indicating stack-shape preservation was needed for matching those erase-screen callbacks.
Used GET_COBJ and GET_JOBJ macros to reproduce object access patterns for HSD_CObjAnim and HSD_JObjAnimAll.
Preserved exact float/frame comparisons such as checking 190.0f against cobj->eyepos->aobj->curr_frame and comparing curr_frame to end_frame.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining un_8031D030 through un_8031Fxxx vi symbols.
Check whether vi_EraseScreen remained inline or was later adjusted for matching/code-size reasons.
Search for later fixes around erase_colors_vi0501 sizing or usage, since this PR names the sbss object and uses it in screen-erasure code.
Look for later PRs that give semantic names to vi_8031C9B4, vi_8031CA04, and vi_8031CAAC.
Audit remaining asm/melee/vi files for stale calls to old un_8031C99C/un_8031C9B4/un_8031CA04/un_8031CAAC names.
